segments.text: Now it came to pass upon a day, that Jonathan the son of Saul said unto the young man that bare his armour,
Come, and let us go over to the Philistines’ garrison, that is on the other side.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: But he told not his father.

footnotes: it came…: or, there was a day

segments.text: And Saul tarried in the uttermost part of Gibeah under a pomegranate tree which is in Migron: and the people that were with him were about six hundred men;
And Ahiah, the son of Ahitub, I-chabod’s brother, the son of Phinehas, the son of Eli, the LORD’s priest in Shiloh, wearing an ephod.
And the people knew not that Jonathan was gone.

And between the passages, by which Jonathan sought to go over unto the Philistines’ garrison, there was a sharp rock on the one side, and a sharp rock on the other side: and the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.
The forefront of the one was situate northward over against Michmash, and the other southward over against Gibeah.
Come, and let us go over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised: it may be that the LORD will work for us: for there is no restraint to the LORD to save by many or by few.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: Do all that is in thine heart: turn thee; behold, I am with thee according to thy heart.
segments.speaker: Armourbearer

segments.text: Behold, we will pass over unto these men, and we will discover ourselves unto them.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: If they say thus unto us, Tarry until we come to you; then we will stand still in our place, and will not go up unto them.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: But if they say thus, Come up unto us; then we will go up: for the LORD hath delivered them into our hand: and this shall be a sign unto us.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: And both of them discovered themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines: and the Philistines said,
Behold, the Hebrews come forth out of the holes where they had hid themselves.
segments.speaker: Philistines

segments.text: Come up after me: for the LORD hath delivered them into the hand of Israel.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: And Jonathan climbed up upon his hands and upon his feet, and his armourbearer after him: and they fell before Jonathan; and his armourbearer slew after him.
And that first slaughter, which Jonathan and his armourbearer made, was about twenty men, within as it were an half acre of land, which a yoke of oxen might plow.

footnotes: an…: or, half a furrow of an acre of land

segments.text: And there was trembling in the host, in the field, and among all the people: the garrison, and the spoilers, they also trembled, and the earth quaked: so it was a very great trembling.

footnotes: a very…: Heb. a trembling of God

segments.text: And the watchmen of Saul in Gibeah of Benjamin looked; and, behold, the multitude melted away, and they went on beating down one another.
Number now, and see who is gone from us.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And when they had numbered, behold, Jonathan and his armourbearer were not there.
Bring hither the ark of God. For the ark of God was at that time with the children of Israel.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And it came to pass, while Saul talked unto the priest, that the noise that was in the host of the Philistines went on and increased: and Saul said unto the priest,
Withdraw thine hand.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And Saul and all the people that were with him assembled themselves, and they came to the battle: and, behold, every man’s sword was against his fellow, and there was a very great discomfiture.

Moreover the Hebrews that were with the Philistines before that time, which went up with them into the camp from the country round about, even they also turned to be with the Israelites that were with Saul and Jonathan.
Likewise all the men of Israel which had hid themselves in mount Ephraim, when they heard that the Philistines fled, even they also followed hard after them in the battle.
So the LORD saved Israel that day: and the battle passed over unto Beth-aven.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And the men of Israel were distressed that day: for Saul had adjured the people, saying,
Cursed be the man that eateth any food until evening, that I may be avenged on mine enemies.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: So none of the people tasted any food.
And all they of the land came to a wood; and there was honey upon the ground.
And when the people were come into the wood, behold, the honey dropped; but no man put his hand to his mouth: for the people feared the oath.

But Jonathan heard not when his father charged the people with the oath: wherefore he put forth the end of the rod that was in his hand, and dipped it in an honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes were enlightened.
Thy father straitly charged the people with an oath, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food this day. And the people were faint.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My father hath troubled the land: see, I pray you, how mine eyes have been enlightened, because I tasted a little of this honey.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: How much more, if haply the people had eaten freely to day of the spoil of their enemies which they found? for had there not been now a much greater slaughter among the Philistines?
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: And they smote the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon: and the people were very faint.
And the people flew upon the spoil, and took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people did eat them with the blood.
Behold, the people sin against the LORD, in that they eat with the blood.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Ye have transgressed: roll a great stone unto me this day.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Disperse yourselves among the people, and say unto them, Bring me hither every man his ox, and every man his sheep, and slay them here, and eat; and sin not against the LORD in eating with the blood.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And all the people brought every man his ox with him that night, and slew them there.

footnotes: with him: Heb. in his hand

segments.text: And Saul built an altar unto the LORD: the same was the first altar that he built unto the LORD.

footnotes: the same…: Heb. that altar he began to build unto the LORD

segments.text: Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and spoil them until the morning light, and let us not leave a man of them.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Do whatsoever seemeth good unto thee.
segments.speaker: People

segments.text: Let us draw near hither unto God.
segments.speaker: Priest

segments.text: Shall I go down after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hand of Israel?
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But he answered him not that day.
Draw ye near hither, all the chief of the people: and know and see wherein this sin hath been this day.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: For, as the LORD liveth, which saveth Israel, though it be in Jonathan my son, he shall surely die.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: But there was not a man among all the people that answered him.
Be ye on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Do what seemeth good unto thee.
segments.speaker: People

segments.text: Give a perfect lot.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And Saul and Jonathan were taken: but the people escaped.

footnotes: Give…: or, Shew the innocent; escaped: Heb. went forth

segments.text: Cast lots between me and Jonathan my son.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: And Jonathan was taken.
Tell me what thou hast done.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: I did but taste a little honey with the end of the rod that was in mine hand, and, lo, I must die.
segments.speaker: Jonathan

segments.text: God do so and more also: for thou shalt surely die, Jonathan.
segments.speaker: Saul

segments.text: Shall Jonathan die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? God forbid: as the LORD liveth, there shall not one hair of his head fall to the ground; for he hath wrought with God this day.
segments.speaker: People

segments.text: So the people rescued Jonathan, that he died not.
Then Saul went up from following the Philistines: and the Philistines went to their own place.

So Saul took the kingdom over Israel, and fought against all his enemies on every side, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines: and whithersoever he turned himself, he vexed them.
And he gathered an host, and smote the Amalekites, and delivered Israel out of the hands of them that spoiled them.
Now the sons of Saul were Jonathan, and Ishui, and Melchi-shua: and the names of his two daughters were these; the name of the firstborn Merab, and the name of the younger Michal:
And the name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam, the daughter of Ahimaaz: and the name of the captain of his host was Abner, the son of Ner, Saul’s uncle.
And Kish was the father of Saul; and Ner the father of Abner was the son of Abiel.
And there was sore war against the Philistines all the days of Saul: and when Saul saw any strong man, or any valiant man, he took him unto him.
